Title: A R2R3‑MYB gene‑based marker for the non‑darkening seed coat trait in pinto and cranberry beans (Phaseolus vulgaris L.) derived from ‘Wit‑rood boontje’

Regulation_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: MYB-like DNA-binding protein; R2R3-MYB family; conserved R2 and R3 DNA-binding repeats; bHLH-interaction motif [D/E]Lx2[R/K]x3Lx6Lx3R; C-terminal EDLL-like transactivation motif
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Cause postharvest seed coat darkening through accumulation and subsequent oxidation in the seed coat
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes postharvest seed coat darkening by enabling proanthocyanidin accumulation; loss-of-function allele confers non-darkening seed coat
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Candidate causal gene for the classical J locus controlling postharvest darkening; tightly associated with the non-darkening trait and proposed as the key activator of seed coat proanthocyanidin biosynthesis
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Likely activates expression of PA biosynthetic genes such as PvANR1 and PvLAR; the frameshift allele likely loses this activation capacity
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: Proposed to function as part of the MBW complex with bHLH and WD40 proteins
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: Seed development; postharvest darkening genetic background
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: QTL-guided candidate gene resequencing; amplicon sequencing across six genotypes; polymorphism association analysis; in silico protein domain and frameshift analysis; homolog comparison to MtPAR; co-segregation of STS marker PvJ-254 in 128 RILs and 38 bean lines/varieties
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Among 21 candidate genes in the major QTL on Pv10, only Phvul.010G130600 contained a coding-region polymorphism consistently associated with the non-darkening phenotype. 2. The non-darkening genotype ‘Wit-rood boontje’ carried a single-base deletion (c.703delG) causing a frameshift (A235fsX271) predicted to disrupt the C-terminal transactivation region/EDLL motif while leaving DNA-binding domains intact. 3. The encoded protein is a R2R3-MYB highly similar to Medicago MtPAR, a regulator of proanthocyanidin biosynthesis. 4. A dominant STS marker based on the wild-type allele showed no recombination with the trait in the tested materials, strongly supporting Phvul.010G130600 as the gene underlying J-mediated seed coat darkening.
Regulation_Genes.Omics_Data: Targeted resequencing / amplicon sequencing (Illumina MiSeq); accession NA
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Recessive non-darkening allele also decreases intensity of background seed coat color according to prior genetic description of j
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The study infers regulatory function from sequence homology and association, but does not directly perform transactivation or expression assays for Phvul.010G130600. The marker PvJ-254 is useful for marker-assisted selection of non-darkening beans in early generations.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Use PvJ-254 or causal-variant-based genotyping for marker-assisted selection of the recessive non-darkening j allele in pinto and cranberry bean breeding; suitable for early-generation selection before seed phenotype is visible; gene editing of the C-terminal activation domain could be a strategy to generate non-darkening seed coats, but possible changes in background seed coat color should be monitored.
